strengths: Built and deployed production-grade agentic workflows at re:cinq using MCP frameworks, directly aligning with the need for tool-augmented, stateful agents beyond prompt chaining.

Demonstrated deep distributed systems expertise through high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby (millions of req/sec) and real-time inference systems using DLang, asynchronous I/O, and caching — matching core fintech infrastructure demands.

Proven track record scaling technical ownership and culture via open-source leadership (GISCollective), CI/CD infrastructure setup, and mentoring (Selenium course, Code Katas), reflecting required engineering leadership beyond individual contribution.
concerns: No explicit evidence of fintech infrastructure experience (credit systems, payment flows, regulatory compliance) — critical for AI-native risk and capital allocation systems.
Limited visibility into multi-agent system architecture design at scale; current role mentions contribution but not ownership of modular frameworks, shared memory, or feedback loops.
German language proficiency is basic, potentially limiting stakeholder alignment in Berlin-based operations despite team location.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare, verifiable production experience in building agentic AI workflows and high-throughput distributed systems that directly map to the core technical demands of the role — particularly in event-driven architectures, real-time inference, and engineering culture — while his open-source leadership and tooling contributions signal strong architectural ownership and scalability mindset, though the absence of fintech-specific domain exposure remains a material gap that must be mitigated through onboarding or complementary team capabilities.
